issue.title: Inquiry Regarding Website Access Issue in AdGuard Environment

labels.name: A: Waiting for data
labels.description: Waiting for more information from the user

issue.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I checked the documentation and found no answer;
- [x] I checked to make sure that this issue has not already been filed;
- [x] This is not an ad/bug report.

### Problem description

We operate an advertising network called ACROSS ADN, and we are reaching out because one of our clients reported an issue related to AdGuard while attempting to run advertisements through our network.

After installing the ADN script on the following website, users who are using AdGuard experienced an issue where the website could not be accessed:

https://www.adventurer.co.kr/

At the moment, the ADN script has been removed from the website, and the site is now accessible again.

Below are the environments where the access issue was observed.

[Access Issue]

PC

Website cannot be accessed in browsers with the AdGuard extension installed

iOS

Website cannot be accessed in browsers such as Safari and Chrome

Android

Website cannot be accessed in browsers such as Samsung Internet and Chrome

We would like to ask the following:

Is it possible that a website becomes inaccessible in an AdGuard environment due to an advertising network script?

If this situation can occur, are there any recommended ways to prevent or resolve this issue?

For your reference, we have attached the ADN script file.

We would appreciate your review and any guidance you can provide.

[ADN Script Integration Guide (1).docx](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26199385/ADN.Script.Integration.Guide.1.docx)


### Proposed solution

If this situation can occur, are there any recommended ways to prevent or resolve this issue?

### Additional information

_No response_

### Issue URL (Ads) [https://platform.twitter.com/widgets](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplatform.twitter.com%2Fwidgets) ### Comment > iOS AdGuard Pro에서 차단 목록에 ||platform.twitter.com^$important 규칙을 추가했지만, platform.twitter.com이 CNAME으로 platform.twitter.map.fastly.net을 가리키고 있어서 A(IPv4) 요청이 차단되지 않고 IP가 반환됩니다. ||platform.twitter.map.fastly.net^$important 도 추가했습니다. 하지만, HTTPS 요청은 DNS 응답이 비어있지만 A 레코드가 통과하면서 실제 연결이 이루어집니다. $dnstype=A 수정자도 iOS/ipadOS에서 작동하지 않는 것 같습니다. 이전에는 정상적으로 차단되었으나 갑자기 차단이 이루어지지 않습니다. ### Screenshots

review.body:
The site is a link shortener with anti-adblock detection, and a dedicated rule was already added for it.
I couldn't trace the original history of why financialtrust.info was added to the shared rules below (referenced under issues #206376, #196297, and #159034):

```adblock
neworldtravel.com,financialtrust.info,...#%#//scriptlet('prevent-fetch', 'pagead2.googlesyndication.com')
neworldtravel.com,financialtrust.info,...#%#//scriptlet('prevent-fetch', '/ads-twitter\.com|pagead|googleads|doubleclick/', '', 'opaque')
neworldtravel.com,financialtrust.info,...#%#//scriptlet('prevent-fetch', 'method:HEAD')
```

None of those three referenced issues is about `financialtrust.info` directly, so I was unable to determine whether removing it from these shared rules would break anything or if the new dedicated rule fully covers its behavior. 

Should I remove it, or leave it in place until the original reason can be confirmed?

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 4 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
Not reproduced on my end.

Please try following actions, then check the problem again:
1. Disable all content blockers in Safari settings and enable them again
2. Restart Safari
3. Restart AdGuard
4. Check for filters update in AdGuard.

### Important:
<!-- **AdGuard for iOS** -->
* Do not close AdGuard window ~60 seconds, because iOS will break configuration process.
<!-- **AdGuard for Safari** -->
* The filters update process is fully completed ~10 seconds after the manual filter update check is completed.

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: Fix AdThrive/Raptive

comment.body:
What is the use case for

```adblock
#@#.content_ad
#@#.content-ad
#@#.banner-ad
```

?

---

`#%#//scriptlet('set-cookie', '__adblocker', 'false') + #%#//scriptlet('prevent-xhr', 'ads.adthrive.com')` is still useful for other platforms as well. Why just restricts it under `!#if (adguard_app_ios && adguard_ext_safari && adguard_ext_android_cb)`?

Android content blocker (`adguard_ext_android_cb`) can't use scriptlets anyways.

pinned_comment.body:
Adguard 브라우저 확장 프로그램 MV3을 사용하시고 숲 광고 차단이 안되는 분들께:
해당 이슈는 최근 변경 사항 ( https://github.com/List-KR/List-KR/commit/29e530df948a1054fba818a1917992c80c3b2d04 )이 Adguard 브라우저 확장 MV3에 아직 적용되지 않아서 발생한 현상입니다.
따라서, Adguard 브라우저 확장 MV3를 사용하시는 분들은 아래 방법을 따라주시기 바랍니다:
 1. 각 브라우저의 웹 확장 프로그램 관리 페이지에 들어갑니다. (예: `chrome://extensions/`)
 2. Adguard 브라우저 확장 프로그램의 디테일 버튼을 선택합니다.
 3. 사용자 스크립트 권한을 부여합니다.
 4. 브라우저 툴바에 있는 Adguard 브라우저 확장 프로그램을 클릭하고 필터 리스트 업데이트 버튼을 누릅니다.
 5. 숲 웹 페이지를 새로고침합니다.
 6. 그럼에도 해결되지 않으면 다음 정식 Adguard 브라우저 확장 MV3의 릴리즈 시기까지 기다리시고 확장 프로그램 자체를 업데이트하셔야 합니다.

이러한 번거러움은 Google의 정책에서 비롯되었습니다.

issue.title: Fix AdThrive/Raptive

comment.body:
> generic general rule seems like the kind of thing that would upset Alex.

Generic network exception is what we try to avoid because that compromises users' privacy. Generic `:not` for cosmetic rules is usually fine because cosmetic rules are meant to be "cosmetic" and usually don't mean much differences in terms of privacy.

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body: I thought about using `:not` or `:has`. however, I don't think iOS free supports them, and making it a generic general rule seems like the kind of thing that would upset Alex.

issue.title: Fix AdThrive/Raptive

comment.body:
I think this better be solved with generic rules rather than per-site.

@Yuki2718 Sorry, can you help with this? Maybe

```adblock
#@#.content_ad
#@#.content-ad
#@#.banner-ad

##.content_ad:not(.text-ad)
##.content-ad:not(.text-ad)
##.banner-ad:not(.text-ad)
```

?

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: Fix AdThrive/Raptive

comment.body:
I just tested with `Advanced protection` turning off and

```adblock
#@#.content_ad
#@#.content-ad
#@#.banner-ad
```

and the site works. When I add

```adblock
##.content_ad:not(.test)
```

the site triggers AS so I think `##.content_ad:not(.test)` should work for iOS free users.

issue.title: Fix AdThrive/Raptive

comment.body:
> however, I don't think iOS free supports them

`:not()` with `##` should be understood as native CSS so I think iOS free version should support it? @Alex-302 Sorry, can you take a look?

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/List-KR/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/List-KR/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/List-KR/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/List-KR/tinyShield/issues).

review.body:
The site is a link shortener with anti-adblock detection, and a dedicated rule was already added for it.
I couldn't trace the original history of why financialtrust.info was added to the shared rules below (referenced under issues #206376, #196297, and #159034):

```adblock
neworldtravel.com,financialtrust.info,...#%#//scriptlet('prevent-fetch', 'pagead2.googlesyndication.com')
neworldtravel.com,financialtrust.info,...#%#//scriptlet('prevent-fetch', '/ads-twitter\.com|pagead|googleads|doubleclick/', '', 'opaque')
neworldtravel.com,financialtrust.info,...#%#//scriptlet('prevent-fetch', 'method:HEAD')
```

None of those three referenced issues is about `financialtrust.info` directly, so I was unable to determine whether removing it from these shared rules would break anything or if the new dedicated rule fully covers its behavior. 

Should I remove it, or leave it in place?
